article_title: KAIST's VOTP Teaches Robots Reward Functions From as Few as Ten Preference Videos, Earning an ICML 2026 Oral
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: KAIST's VOTP Teaches Robots Reward Functions From as Few as Ten Preference Videos, Earning an ICML 2026 Oral
content_preview.summary: A KAIST team uses optimal transport over video-foundation-model embeddings to learn robot rewards from a handful of preference labels, winning a top-0.7% ICML 2026 oral slot.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A team at the Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST) has developed a method that teaches robots which behaviors a human prefers using only a handful of labeled video comparisons, rather than the thousands to tens of thousands of human judgments that preference-based learning usually demands. The technique, called VOTP — Video-based Optimal Transport Preference — has been accepted as an oral presentation at the International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML) 2...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong Analysis piece (1,007 words, within the 800-2,000 range). Clean Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis structure. Technical concepts (PbRL, optimal transport, video foundation models, pseudo-labeling) are explained accurately and accessibly without overstating the result. The 'What We Don't Know' section is exemplary: it explicitly flags the unnamed foundation model, the missing benchmark/baseline names, the absent margins, and the single-outlet provenance of the real-robot numbers.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 source snapshots read from disk and verified. source-0 (Mirage News, status 200): confirms author roles/affiliations ('Tung M. Luu, PhD candidate, first author'; 'Hwanhee Kim, M.S candidate, second author'; 'Professor Chang D. Yoo'; School of Electrical Engineering), ICML 2026 at COEX Seoul in July, and the top-0.7%/168/23,918 stat. source-1 (icml.cc official program, status 200): paper title verbatim, full abstract present, all four abstract quotes ('its scalability is hindered by high labeling costs'; 'a semi-supervised framework that learns effective reward functions from only a handful of labels'; 'leveraging optimal transport to align visual trajectories within the rich representation space of' ViFMs; 'high-fidelity pseudo-labels for large amounts of unlabeled data'; 'outperforms state-of-the-art offline PbRL methods under limited feedback budgets'; 'visual distractors') reproduced verbatim, VOTP expansion 'Video-based Optimal Transport Preference' confirmed, first author listed as 'Minh-Tung Luu'. source-2 (OpenReview, status 200): all seven keywords confirmed verbatim and in the exact order the article lists them; first author rendered 'Tung Minh Luu'. source-3 (The Brighter Side of News, status 200): every experimental specific verbatim — 50 demonstrations, keyboard teleoperation, 50 percent success rate, five preference labels for LiftBanana, 10 for DrawerOpen, VOTP 80% / 70% — plus the 168/23,918/0.7% figures and the Yoo quote 'The core of physical AI is making machines understand human intentions and choose the correct actions.' source-4 (Seoul Economic Daily, status 200): 'around 10 good demonstration videos and bad demonstration videos' quote verbatim and the applications list (robotic arm, humanoid, autonomous vehicles, smart factories, drones, surgical robots, screen-viewing software agents). Rule 9 satisfied: abstract quotes are cited to ICML/OpenReview; experimental specifics are cited to the outlet (Brighter Side / Seoul Economic Daily) carrying them verbatim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No hallucinations detected. Critically, the spurious arXiv ID 2602.11393 (a separate Verghese/Atkeson paper) does NOT appear anywhere in the article or in any of the five source snapshots — confirmed absent. The author-name discrepancy (ICML: 'Minh-Tung Luu'; KAIST/Mirage: 'Tung M. Luu'; OpenReview: 'Tung Minh Luu') is openly disclosed in the 'What We Don't Know' section rather than silently resolved, satisfying Rule 7. The one recoverable issue: the body sentence in 'What We Know' asserts 'Tung M. Luu is the first author' (the KAIST rendering) while the authoritative ICML program lists 'Minh-Tung Luu' first — a clarification note makes the canonical first-author rendering explicit for readers.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. Efficiency claims are consistently hedged ('if the efficiency results hold up under independent scrutiny'; 'readers should treat those specifics as preliminary'). No sensationalism, no AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior coverage of VOTP, KAIST, or preference-based reinforcement learning in the archive. No overlap with recent robotics coverage (e.g., the China humanoid-robots deployment article is unrelated).
concerns: Four of five source domains (icml.cc, openreview.net, thebrighterside.news, en.sedaily.com) are not in config/source_allowlist.txt, producing the automated warning. icml.cc and openreview.net are the authoritative primary sources for the paper; Brighter Side and Seoul Economic Daily are the only outlets carrying the real-robot numbers verbatim. Per policy the allowlist is not modified during review; the warning is recorded but does not undermine verifiability since every snapshot returned 200 and confirmed its attributed claims.

The headline real-robot numbers (5/10 labels, 80%/70% success) trace to a single trade outlet's account of the paper rather than the conference abstract. The article already discloses this limitation explicitly in 'What We Don't Know.'
recommendations: Filing a corrections record to make the canonical first-author rendering ('Minh-Tung Luu' per the official ICML program) explicit, since the body asserts the 'Tung M. Luu' rendering as first author.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully source-verified Analysis. Paper identity, author names, and experimental numbers all check out against the snapshots; the wrong arXiv paper was correctly excluded and the author-name variance was honestly disclosed. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS: the single recoverable item — the body's first-author rendering vs the official ICML program — is honestly summarizable in one corrections note, and the article publishes with that public trail.
